tactical_shape: Dominant control through Rodri and Pedri, with Olmo finding pockets between lines
standout_players: Lamine Yamal: scored inside 10 minutes on his first start since hamstring injury; ever-present threat and set the tone
Mikel Oyarzabal: one assist and two goals within 23 minutes; exceptional all-round contribution
Rodri: controlled the match, completing 113 passes
Dani Olmo: found spaces where they seemed not to exist
Pedro Porro: made an impact and provided the curling cross for the third goal
Marc Cucurella: forced the fourth and scored the fourth off a defender
Aymeric Laporte: crucially nodded down for Oyarzabal's second
Unai Simón: little to do, no significant save until the 80th minute
fatigue_injuries: Lamine Yamal was withdrawn at 45 minutes, having just returned from a hamstring injury in April
Oyarzabal was also taken off at half-time to protect him
De la Fuente made changes early to protect players and give minutes to others
home_read.momentum: Spain were 'Spain again' — response to criticism was emphatic, confidence restored, and the performance sends a strong statement going forward.

tactical_shape: Deeply unable to cope with Spain's intensity and wide overloads
standout_players: Salem al-Dawsari: led the rare Saudi attack and was involved in the early battles with Lamine Yamal
Mohammed al-Owais: beaten four times; also denied Oyarzabal's curling effort by the bar
away_read.momentum: Overrun and exposed, with little evidence in the punditry of a positive immediate takeaway beyond the experience of facing elite opposition.
